channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ukrainians disappointed after Trump-Zelenskyy meeting fails to immediately secure Tomahawk missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainians shared their disappointment Saturday that the U.S. may not provide Kyiv with long-range Tomahawk missiles, while work to repair the damaged power supply to the country's Zaporizhzhia power plant soothed other concerns surrounding Europe's largest nuclear plant.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The most recent torrential rains in east-central Mexico, which have left at least 76 dead and dozens more missing, have raised questions again about the government’s ability to alert people to severe weather in time.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US Embassy issues warning to Americans in Trinidad and Tobago as tensions with Venezuela escalate
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Trinidad and Tobago is cautioning Americans to stay away from U.S. government facilities on the twin-island nation.
item.dc:creator: Anselm Gibbs, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelensky says Trump didn’t say ‘yes’ to Tomahawk missiles request
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday said President Trump poured cold water on his request to provide the Eastern European nation with Tomahawk missiles amid its over three-years-long war with Russia. “It's very difficult just to operate only with Ukrainian drones.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A look at Prince Andrew's antics and scandals that have tried royal patience for decades
item.description: Britain’s Prince Andrew has been forced to relinquish use of his remaining royal titles after the latest revelations about his relationship with the convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein proved one scandal too many for his brother, King Charles III.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gaza famine claims face mounting scrutiny as mortality data falls far short of predictions
item.description: Experts challenge Gaza famine claims by the Integrated Food Security Phase Classification, citing insufficient mortality data and declining food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya, as thousands mourn former Prime Minister Raila Odinga
item.description: Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu before the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: Britain believes its experience in Northern Ireland could help achieve a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Hui And Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel received coffin of missing hostage handed over to Red Cross, prime minister's office says
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel received the remains of another hostage from Gaza on Friday, the prime minister’s office said, a handover from Hamas as the militant group worked to shore up a ceasefire by using bulldozers in a search for bodies under the rubble in the war-scarred enclave.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After years of socialist rule, Bolivia’s presidential runoff tests how far and fast it veers right
item.description: EL ALTO, Bolivia (AP) — After nearly two decades of one-party rule, three years of an accelerating currency crisis and too many months of mind-numbing fuel lines, Bolivia is lurching to the right.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and CARLOS VALDEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump drops f-bomb in Zelensky meeting about Maduro
item.description: "He doesn't want to f‑‑‑ with the U.S.," Trump said, speaking from the White House during a lunch meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Daily struggles persist in Gaza even as ceasefire offers some respite
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Bassil Naggar can finally sleep without getting jolted awake by the sounds of Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: TOQA EZZIDIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas defends executions of Palestinians after Trump issues stern warning to terror group
item.description: After Trump’s threat, Hamas official Mohammed Nazzal defends the recent executions of Palestinians in Gaza as the group seeks to settle scores with opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senators look to block Trump from engaging in ‘hostilities’ in Venezuela
item.description: Senators Tim Kaine and Rand Paul lead bipartisan effort to prevent President Donald Trump from launching military strikes in Venezuela without Congress approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran’s execution rate tops 1,000 this year as death row inmates launch hunger strike
item.description: Iranian death row prisoners call on U.N. Secretary General António Guterres to intervene as the regime executes over 1,000 people since the start of the year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia convicts 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges
item.description: A court in southern Russia convicted 15 captured Ukrainian soldiers on terrorism charges Friday after a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thatcher centennial celebration examines how Reagan partnership guides modern US-UK relations
item.description: Experts examine how the Cold War alliance between President Ronald Reagan and Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher influences today's U.S.-U.K. relationship amid Russian President Vladimir Putin's threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hannibal Gadhafi, son of late Libyan leader, ordered released in Lebanon if he pays $11 million bail
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A Lebanese judge on Friday ordered the release of the son of Libya’s late leader Moammar Gadhafi on condition that he pay $11 million bail. Hannibal Gadhafi has been imprisoned in Lebanon for a decade without being charged.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump is ramping up military pressure on Venezuela’s authoritarian leader Nicolás Maduro, raising the prospect of strikes inside the country, which would mark a major escalation from already controversial military attacks against alleged drug trafficking boats in international waters.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US military drone strike on drug 'submersible' in Caribbean leaves survivors, official confirms
item.description: U.S. military drone strike destroyed a drug-smuggling submersible in the Caribbean Thursday, leaving 2 to 3 survivors in the water, a U.S. official tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN condemns military takeover in Madagascar as coup leader to be quickly sworn in as president
item.description: ANTANANARIVO, Madagascar (AP) — The United Nations chief on Thursday condemned the military takeover in Madagascar as an army colonel who led the coup was set to be quickly sworn in as president just three days after announcing soldiers had taken charge. U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: 'No choice but to go in and kill' Hamas if Gaza bloodshed doesn't stop
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that if Hamas does not heed repeated warnings to stop bloodshed in Gaza, the U.S. will have “no choice” but to use lethal force. “If Hamas continues to kill people in Gaza, which was not the Deal, we will have no choice but to go in and kill them.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Greene rebukes Trump administration’s multibillion-dollar bailout for Argentina
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) sharply criticized the Trump administration on Thursday for seeking to provide as much as $40 billion in financing for Argentina to help the Latin American ally stabilize its turbulent financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump signals new trust in Erdogan, raising concerns over Turkey’s ambitions in Gaza and beyond
item.description: Turkey seeks expanded influence in Gaza through construction contracts and peacekeeping roles as President Recep Tayyip Erdogan rebuilds trust with Washington after the ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Just-released hostage attends funeral of fellow soldier, whose body was among few returned from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Two hostages released by Hamas were reunited Wednesday in a Jerusalem cemetery for a final goodbye. Surrounded by hundreds of mourners, Matan Angrest, who had returned to Israel just two days earlier, stood before the freshly dug grave cradling his 22-year-old commander, Capt.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel receives remains of 2 more hostages as military says another body was not that of a hostage
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel received the remains of two more hostages Wednesday, hours after the Israeli military said that one of the bodies previously turned over was not that of a hostage. The confusion added to tensions over the fragile truce that has paused the two-year war.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukrainian officials meet with US weapons manufacturers before Trump-Zelenskyy talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian government delegation has met with prominent American weapons manufacturers during a U.S. visit, a senior Kyiv official said Wednesday, before President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s meeting with U.S. counterpart Donald Trump at the White House later this week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A look at some world leaders who have fled uprisings
item.description: A military coup this week in Madagascar forced the president into exile, joining a list of international leaders who fled their countries or went into hiding during uprisings to avoid incarceration or retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Gumede And Mogomotsi Magome, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan announce ceasefire after civilians killed
item.description: ISLAMABAD/KABUL, Oct 15 (Reuters) - Pakistan and Afghanistan agreed to a "temporary ceasefire" on Wednesday, Islamabad said, after an airstrike and ground fighting sent tensions between the South Asian neighbors soaring, killing more than a dozen civilians.
item.dc:creator: By Saleem Ahmed, Mushtaq Ali and Mohammad Yunus Yawar, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese airlines protest US plan to ban their flights over Russian airspace
item.description: The U.S. side has said such flights give Chinese airlines an unfair cost advantage over American carriers, which cannot cross through Russian airspace.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's power worldwide has 'increased 1,000%': Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Sunday marked 737 days since the hostages were taken, and all 20 hostages remaining alive were released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Living hostages and Palestinian prisoners freed as Trump tours region
item.description: Hamas released all 20 of the last living hostages on Monday, the Israeli military said, as part of a breakthrough ceasefire after two years of war between Israel and Hamas in the devastated Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel will exile most released Palestinian prisoners with life sentences
item.description: At least 154 prisoners, most of whom have been convicted for attacks on Israelis, will be deported, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza photojournalist says his disabled brother is missing
item.description: A Gaza-based freelance photojournalist said his disabled brother went missing in August after seeking aid. The family hoped he would be returned today.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to Knesset: Israel has won ‘all that they can’ in military campaign
item.description: President Trump on Monday urged Israeli leaders to turn the page from warfare to peace, telling the Knesset that Israel had won “all that they can by force of arms” and expressing optimism about the region’s future.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: From a hilltop village and across Israel: Tears of joy as the living hostages are returned
item.description: LAVON, Israel (AP) — It was a day of joy and tears across Israel. Families, loved ones and friends watched as Hamas released the last living hostages held captive for over two years in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9
